seo.title: AI Agent Observability in Smolagents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent observability in Smolagents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Smolagents, AI agent observability challenges arise from its lightweight design, where agents generate and execute Python code directly with minimal built-in tracing or error boundaries. Common issues like unsafe code execution, tool misinterpretation, and compounding reasoning errors go undetected without explicit instrumentation, as the framework prioritizes simplicity over robust logging. Vex integration via wrapping agent.run() provides essential visibility into these failure modes specific to Smolagents' code-first agent patterns.

howItManifests.scenario: Code agent generating unsafe Python execution
howItManifests.symptom: Agent writes code using __import__('os').system('rm -rf /') without sandboxing
howItManifests.impact: Complete data loss or security breach during agent.run()

howItManifests.symptom: Agent calls calculator tool with 'search web for weather' instead of numeric input
howItManifests.impact: Tool failures cascade, breaking multi-step workflows

howItManifests.symptom: Agent accumulates incorrect intermediate variables across code blocks
howItManifests.impact: Final output diverges exponentially from correct solution

howItManifests.scenario: Minimal error handling in generated code
howItManifests.symptom: try-except blocks missing, causing unhandled ZeroDivisionError
howItManifests.impact: Agent crashes mid-execution with no recovery mechanism

detection.name: Unsafe Code Pattern Scanner
detection.description: Detects dangerous Python constructs like os.system(), eval(), or file deletions in generated agent code

detection.name: Tool Call Validation Monitor
detection.description: Intercepts agent tool calls to verify inputs match tool schemas before execution

detection.name: Reasoning Drift Detector
detection.description: Tracks intermediate variable values across agent code steps to detect compounding errors

fixes.name: Sandbox Agent Execution
fixes.description: Wraps all generated code execution in restricted Python environment

fixes.name: Structured Tool Interface
fixes.description: Enforces Pydantic validation on all tool inputs/outputs

fixes.name: Error Recovery Wrapper
fixes.description: Adds comprehensive try-catch with agent self-healing around codegen

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Observability Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description: Vex automates Smolagents observability by wrapping agent.run() calls with verify(), instantly detecting unsafe code generation, tool misuse, and reasoning drift through specialized detectors trained on Smolagents failure patterns. It provides real-time alerts with code snippets, automatic remediation suggestions, and performance baselines without modifying core agent logic. The integration reveals hidden issues like silent variable corruption across multi-step reasoning that manual logging misses, enabling proactive fixes before production failures.
vexAutomation.code:
from smolagents import Agent, tool
from vex_sdk import verify

@tool
def search(query: str):
    return f"Search results for {query}"

agent = Agent(tools=[search])

# Vex wraps agent.run() automatically
@verify(
    detectors=[
        'python-unsafe-code',
        'tool-schema-violation',
        'reasoning-drift',
        'missing-error-handling'
    ],  
    auto_fix=True
)
def safe_agent_task(task: str):
    result = agent.run(task)
    return result

# Usage
response = safe_agent_task("Calculate 100/0 then search weather")

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install smolagents vex-sdk` and wrap your agent.run() calls today.
